skill.name: mcp-builder
skill.description: Guide for creating MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers. Use this when building integrations with external services, creating new MCP servers, or connecting Claude to APIs.
skill.summary: Guide for creating MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers. Use this when building integrations with ex...
skill.author: Doyajin174

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, network, weak-crypto, and reconnaissance patterns in SKILL.md. Manual review found these are Markdown examples, documentation links, and textual false positives, with no evidence of malicious intent or hidden execution.

low_findings.title: Markdown Examples Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The external command alerts point to Markdown fences, inline code terms, and sample MCP Inspector commands. These examples are relevant to MCP development and do not execute by themselves.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are visible documentation examples or Markdown delimiters. No evidence found of automatic execution, command construction, or user-controlled shell invocation.

low_findings.title: Documentation URLs Misclassified as Network Risk
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs are a sample API-key help URL and public MCP reference links. No evidence found that the skill transmits data to these URLs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs appear only as documentation or example text. There is no fetch, curl command, telemetry endpoint, or credential exfiltration behavior.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are Textual False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings match ordinary words in the skill description, annotations, and sample tool metadata. No cryptographic algorithm use is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or similar weak cryptographic algorithm usage. The matches are unrelated prose or sample schema fields.

low_findings.title: Reconnaissance Alert Is Example Error Documentation
low_findings.description: The system reconnaissance alert points to an example error message that tells users where to get an API key. It does not collect host, user, process, or network details.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is a static TypeScript error-message example. No evidence found of system inventory commands, environment probing, or outbound reporting.

content.user_title: Build MCP Servers for AI Integrations
content.value_statement: Building reliable MCP servers requires clear protocol choices, tool schemas, and evaluation plans. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through practical MCP server design.
actual_capabilities: Explains a four-phase workflow for researching, implementing, testing, and evaluating MCP servers.
Recommends transport choices for local CLI, remote server, and browser contexts.
Shows a TypeScript-oriented MCP server structure with client, tool, and type modules.
Provides guidance for tool naming, annotations, error messages, and stateless design.
Describes evaluation prompts that verify tool behavior in realistic scenarios.
limitations: It is a written guide, not a complete MCP server generator.
Examples are brief and require adaptation for a real API.
It does not include authentication, deployment, or production monitoring details.
Some reference text is Korean, so teams may need translation for shared documentation.

use_cases.title: Plan a New MCP Integration
use_cases.description: Define the target API, transport mode, tool boundaries, and evaluation strategy before implementation begins.

use_cases.title: Review Tool Design
use_cases.description: Check tool names, annotations, input schemas, and error messages for clear model-facing behavior.

use_cases.title: Create MCP Evaluation Questions
use_cases.description: Draft realistic read-only evaluation cases with expected tools and verifiable answers.

prompt_templates.title: Start an MCP Server Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner planning for a first integration.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the mcp-builder skill to plan an MCP server for [service]. Identify the transport, tools, input schemas, and first evaluation cases.

prompt_templates.title: Design Tool Contracts
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate tool design for an existing API.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the mcp-builder skill to design MCP tools for these API operations: [operations]. Include names, annotations, inputs, and actionable error behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Review an MCP Implementation
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate review of a draft server.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the mcp-builder skill to review this MCP server outline: [outline]. Find gaps in transport choice, tool schemas, state management, and testing.

prompt_templates.title: Build an Evaluation Suite
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the mcp-builder skill to create ten complex MCP evaluation questions for [integration]. Keep them read-only, independent, and verifiable.

output_examples.input: Plan an MCP server for a customer support API.
output: Transport recommendation for local and remote usage.
A short list of model-facing tools with clear names and annotations.
Initial evaluation questions for customer lookup and ticket search.

output_examples.input: Review my MCP tool names and error messages.
output: Renaming suggestions that make each tool action explicit.
Notes about destructive or open-world annotations.
Improved error messages that tell the user how to recover.

output_examples.input: Create evaluation cases for a finance reporting integration.
output: Read-only questions that match realistic reporting tasks.
Expected tool usage for each scenario.
Validation criteria based on observable answer properties.

best_practices: Start with the target API and user tasks before choosing tool names.
Use explicit tool annotations for read-only, destructive, idempotent, and open-world behavior.
Create evaluations that are independent, realistic, and easy to verify.
anti_patterns: Do not create vague tools such as process or handle.
Do not hide external side effects behind read-only tool descriptions.
Do not skip MCP Inspector testing before sharing a server.

faq.question: What does this skill help build?
faq.answer: It helps plan and review MCP servers that connect AI assistants to external APIs or services.

faq.question: Does it generate a complete server?
faq.answer: No. It provides architecture, examples, and review guidance that developers adapt into working code.

faq.question: Which implementation language does it emphasize?
faq.answer: It emphasizes TypeScript examples and references the Model Context Protocol SDK.

faq.question: Can it help with testing?
faq.answer: Yes. It recommends MCP Inspector testing and evaluation questions with verifiable expected behavior.

faq.question: Does it cover production deployment?
faq.answer: Only lightly. It discusses transport choices but does not provide full deployment or operations guidance.

faq.question: Why are external command and network risk factors listed?
faq.answer: The skill includes sample terminal commands and documentation URLs. Manual review found no malicious execution or data transfer.
